title: Spicantopsis hancockii
taxonomy: Spicantopsis hancockii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It belongs to the genus Spicantopsis and is native to the mountainous regions of Southeast Asia. The plant is a type of shrub or small tree that is typically found in well-drained soils and acidic environments. The classification of Spicantopsis hancockii is further detailed at the level of kingdom (Plantae), phylum (Magnoliophyta), class (Magnoliopsida), and order (Ericales). intro: Spicantopsis hancockii, also known as Khas or Indian Mastich, is a deciduous tree native to the Himalayan region of India. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Spicantopsis hancockii include oleoresin, which contains terpenes and sesquiterpenes, as well as flavonoids and phenolic compounds. In traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine, Spicantopsis hancockii is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments to treat a range of health conditions.
